content: Therapeutic index (TI) is the ratio between toxic and therapeutic drug concentrations: TI = TD50/ED50 (dose toxic to 50% / dose effective in 50%) or TC50/EC50 for concentrations. Narrow therapeutic index (NTI) drugs have TI <2, requiring careful dosing and monitoring. NTI drugs include: warfarin (INR 2-3 therapeutic, >4 bleeding risk), digoxin (0.5-0.9 ng/mL therapeutic, >1.2 toxicity), lithium (0.6-1.2 mEq/L therapeutic, >1.5 toxicity), phenytoin (10-20 mcg/mL), theophylline (10-20 mcg/mL), aminoglycosides, cyclosporine. Small dose changes or drug interactions can cause toxicity or therapeutic failure. FDA requires bioequivalence standards of 90-111% for NTI drugs vs 80-125% for others.
